data.title: Lithium Chile announces upsized life offering of up to $6.5M
data.description:
• Lithium Chile (TSXV:LITH:CA) said on Thursday that it has agreed with its agents, Canaccord Genuity and Moneta Securities, to increase the size of the previously announced "best efforts" private placement. 
  • As a result of this amendment, the offering will consist of up to 13M units of the company at a price of $0.50 per unit for aggregate gross proceeds of up to $6.5M. 
  • The company has also granted the agents an option, exercisable in whole or in part at any time prior to the closing of the offering, to arrange for the sale of up to an additional 1.95M units at the offering price, for additional gross proceeds of up to $975,000.
  • The net proceeds of the offering will be used to advance the company's existing mineral properties in Argentina and Chile, for general and administrative expenses, and for working capital purposes.

data.title: NGEx receives court approval for the spin-out of royalties
data.description:
• NGEx Minerals (TSX:NGEX:CA) said on Thursday that it has obtained a final order from the Supreme Court of British Columbia approving the previously announced spin-out transaction pursuant to which the company will spin-out net smelter returns royalties on the Lunahuasi and Los Helados projects into a wholly-owned subsidiary of NGEx, LunR Royalties. 
  • It is anticipated that NGEx shareholders holding NGEx shares as of the business day immediately prior to the effective date of the arrangement will be entitled to receive the New NGEx shares and the LunR Royalties shares pursuant to the arrangement.

data.title: BOJ leaves interest rate steady at 0.5% as expected
data.description:
The Bank of Japan left its benchmark short-term interest rate steady at 0.5% during the September 2025 meeting, maintaining borrowing costs at the highest level recorded since 2008 and in line with market expectations.

The policy rate remaining at 0.5% signals the continuation of the Bank of Japan's cautious approach to tightening after years of ultra-low and negative interest rates. 

 At Friday's meeting, the BoJ announced it would begin selling its holdings of exchange-traded funds ((ETFs)) and real-estate investment trusts ((REITs)).

The board noted Japan’s economy had recovered moderately despite some weakness. Private consumption was resilient, helped by improving employment and income. Exports and industrial output remained subdued, with front-loading ahead of U.S. tariff hikes and a subsequent pullback.

Separately, Japan's annual inflation rate eased to 2.7% in August 2025 from 3.1% in the previous month, marking the lowest reading since October 2024.

data.title: Rithm Capital prices public offering of preferred stock
data.description:
• Rithm Capital (NYSE:RITM) said on Thursday that it has priced an underwritten public offering of 7.6M shares of its 8.75% Series E Fixed-Rate Cumulative Redeemable Preferred Stock with a liquidation preference of $25 per share, for gross proceeds of approximately $190M. 
  • The company intends to apply to list the preferred stock on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol “RITM PR E.” 
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from this offering for investments and general corporate purposes.

data.title: Grupo Aeroportuario announces credit line refinancing for $40M
data.description:
• Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacífico (NYSE:PAC) announced on Thursday the refinancing of a USD $40M credit line for a five-year term with the same financial institution. 
  • Interest will be payable monthly at a variable rate equivalent to SOFR plus 81 basis points, with no additional commissions. 
  • The payment of the principal will be due at maturity, on September 18, 2030.

data.title: AvePoint announces dual listing on the Singapore exchange
data.description:
•  AvePoint (NASDAQ:AVPT) announced on Thursday its listing on the Main Board of the Singapore Exchange Securities Trading Limited under the symbol “AVP.” 
  • AvePoint established its initial presence in Singapore in 2009, and Singapore now serves as the company’s Asia Headquarters and International R&D Hub.

data.title: Japan's inflation eases to 2.7% in August
data.description:
• Japan's annual inflation rate eased to 2.7% in August 2025 from 3.1% in the previous month, marking the lowest reading since October 2024.
  • The slowdown was driven mainly by more significant drops in electricity prices, thanks to government subsidies, and a fall in gas prices.
  • Inflation accelerated for housing, clothing, transport, communications, and miscellaneous goods.
  • On the food side, prices rose 7.2%, easing from July’s five-month peak of 7.6%, driven by the smallest gain in rice prices in eight months amid Tokyo’s ongoing efforts to curb staple food costs.
  • Core inflation, which excludes fresh food but includes energy, also stood at 2.7% annually, matching market expectations and reaching a nine-month low.
  • On a monthly basis, the CPI edged up 0.1%, holding steady for the third straight month.

data.title: Reviva Pharmaceuticals announces pricing of $9M public offering
data.description:
• Reviva Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:RVPH) announced on Thursday a public offering with existing and new healthcare-focused institutional investors for the purchase and sale of 27M shares of its common stock together with Series E warrants and Series F warrants, at a combined offering price of $0.335 per share, for gross proceeds of approximately $9M.  
  • The Series E Warrants and the Series F Warrants will have an exercise price of $0.335 per share. 
  • The company currently intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to fund research and development activities and for working capital and other general corporate purposes.

data.title: Ventas declares $0.48 dividend
data.description:
• Ventas (NYSE:VTR) declares $0.48/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.82%
  • Payable Oct. 16; for shareholders of record Sept. 30; ex-div Sept. 30.
  • See VTR Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Friday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count 
 
The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs. Used for drilling wellbores for wells that may eventually produce oil or gas, active rigs are essential for the exploration and development of oil and gas fields. 
 
2:30 PM Fed's Daly Speech 
 
San Francisco Federal Reserve Bank President Mary Daly will participate in fireside chat on AI implications for workforce development and economic mobility hosted by the Emerging Tech Economic Research Network.
